key_insight: Adding SageMath as a verifiable tool in a ReAct-style LLM agent substantially improves performance on research-level math tasks and helps close the gap between open and closed models.
engineering_relevance: This suggests CAS-augmented agent workflows can better support computational mathematics, experimental exploration, and future automated conjecture discovery.
method: Built a ReAct agent that pairs LLM reasoning with SageMath for executable, verifiable feedback.
Added Context7 to retrieve up-to-date documentation during tool use.
Refined the RealMath benchmark with multi-step post-processing and multi-stage validation to improve problem-set quality.
evidence_signal: Across frontier models on RealMath, SageMath access improved average solve rates by 9.7 percentage points, with gains from 1.5 to 27.8 points; GPT-5.5 reached 75.2% solve rate and the lowest token use among tool-enabled setups.
watch_item: Results depend on benchmark extraction and validation quality, so the reported gains may be sensitive to the new filtering pipeline and tool availability.
paper_title: Evaluating SageMath-Augmented LLM Agents for Computational and Experimental Mathematics
